# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 111

This is turn 111. Turns 1 through 110 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 111.

## Turn 111 — all six lemmas are relative to one common boundary object

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the proof, the exponent is the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is only its verifier residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

Turn 110 repaired the scalar source-domain issue by replacing a literal common character with a boundary map \(\bar\psi:\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\to E\). The clean formulation is stronger: the tame and scalar frames are the two projections of one common boundary group. 

### 1. The common boundary fiber product

Let \(T\) be the common tame quotient and let

\[
\Pi=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\ 
x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}
\]

be the common marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient. Put

\[
\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}=\Pi/\Phi(\Pi)\simeq C_2^3.
\]

There are canonical maps

\[
\epsilon_T:T\longrightarrow C_2,\qquad
\epsilon_\Pi:\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\longrightarrow C_2,
\]

both recording the parity of \(\sigma\). The first is the unique nontrivial elementary \(2\)-quotient of \(T\): in any elementary abelian \(2\)-quotient, the tame relation gives

\[
\tau=\tau^2=1,
\]

so only \(\sigma\) survives.

Define

\[
\partial:=T\times_{C_2}\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}.
\tag{111.1}
\]

For either source

\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_A,\Gamma_F=G_{\mathbb Q_2}\},
\]

let \(t_\Gamma:\Gamma\to T\) and \(s_\Gamma:\Gamma\to\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\) be the tame and elementary pro-\(2\) quotient maps. Then

\[
b_\Gamma=(t_\Gamma,s_\Gamma):\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow\partial.
\tag{111.2}
\]

Indeed, its image is a subdirect product of \(T\) and \(\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\). By profinite Goursat, the image is a fiber product over a common quotient. Any common quotient is an elementary abelian \(2\)-quotient of \(T\), hence is at most \(C_2\); the shared unramified quadratic character shows that it is exactly \(C_2\).

Thus both source groups have literally the same boundary quotient \(\partial\).

Now fix

\[
\alpha:T\twoheadrightarrow H,\qquad
\bar\psi:\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}\to E,
\]

and define

\[
\beta_{\alpha,\bar\psi}:\partial\longrightarrow H\times E,
\qquad
(t,p)\longmapsto(\alpha(t),\bar\psi(p)).
\tag{111.3}
\]

For a marked target

\[
Y=(Y,L_Y,\pi_Y,\theta_Y),
\qquad
\ker\pi_Y=L_Y,
\]

write \(q_Y=(\pi_Y,\theta_Y):Y\to H\times E\). The corrected framed count is

\[
e_\Gamma^\beta(Y)=
\#\left\{
f:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow Y:
q_Yf=\beta_{\alpha,\bar\psi}b_\Gamma
\right\}.
\tag{111.4}
\]

This single equation replaces both conditions in Turn 110. Since \(W_\Gamma=\ker t_\Gamma\), it automatically implies

\[
f(W_\Gamma)\le L_Y.
\]

There is no longer a separate compatibility sentence: incompatibility simply means that the set in (111.4) is empty on both sides.

### 2. Audit of the six structural lemmas

#### Lemma 1: terminal scalar pullback

In the terminal branch,

\[
Y\simeq H\times_{H_2}Q
\]

for a finite \(2\)-group \(Q\). If \(N=\ker(H\to H_2)\), then \(N\) has odd order. Every homomorphism \(\theta_Y:Y\to E\) kills \(N\), so it factors uniquely through \(Q\):

\[
\theta_Y=\theta_Q\operatorname{pr}_Q.
\]

A boundary-framed map is therefore exactly a map

\[
\varphi:\Pi\to Q
\]

satisfying the prescribed map to \(H_2\) and

\[
\theta_Q\varphi=\bar\psi\circ(\Pi\to\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}).
\]

Both sources consequently have the identical terminal mapping problem from the same \(\Pi\). The exact-image-poset correspondence with compatible subgroups of \(Q\) also preserves this boundary condition.

#### Lemma 2: active-block collapse

Retain

\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad C=Y/K,\qquad B=Y/R.
\]

Since \(K\le L_Y\), we already have \(\pi_Y(K)=1\). Also,

\[
\theta_Y(R)=0
\]

because \(R=\Phi(K)\), while the established active-block property

\[
(M^\vee)^C=0
\]

gives

\[
\operatorname{Hom}_C(M,E)=0
\]

componentwise. Hence

\[
\theta_Y(K)=0.
\tag{111.5}
\]

Therefore

\[
K\le\ker q_Y.
\]

Both \(q_Y\) and the fixed boundary map descend unchanged to \(B\) and \(C\). The Thompson-critical active-block extraction is consequently internal to the category of \(\partial\)-framed targets.

#### Lemma 3: common-word deformation duality

Let \(A\) be any elementary \(C\)-module occurring inside \(K\), and fix a lower map

\[
\rho:\Gamma\to C
\]

satisfying the boundary equation.

Because \(A\le K\le\ker q_Y\), every lift of \(\rho\) through \(A\) has exactly the same boundary value. Thus the boundary frame does not cut the affine \(Z_\rho^1(A)\)-torsor.

The finite-Heisenberg duality statement therefore applies to the complete framed lift fiber:

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(A)^\vee\simeq(A^\vee)^C,
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(A)
=
2\dim A+\dim(A^\vee)^C.
\tag{111.6}
\]

No extra decorated or boundary-restricted deformation complex is required.

#### Lemma 4: simple-head determinant and shearing

All determinant operations occur inside the boundary kernel.

A shear on a split layer,

\[
(v,c)\longmapsto(v+a(c),c),
\]

adds an element of \(V\le K\), and hence fixes \(q_Y\) by (111.5). It therefore preserves the tame frame, scalar boundary and marking simultaneously.

Likewise, every scalar pushout

\[
\lambda:R\to C_2
\]

produces a central cover whose new kernel is invisible to \(q_Y\). The same holds for the auxiliary central cover representing the inflated class \(\delta\in H^2(C,\mathbb F_2)\): its boundary map is simply pulled back from \(C\).

Thus transgression, filtration-one edge removal, marking-preserving shearing and inflated-class descent are all morphisms over the single boundary \(\partial\). The Fourier characters \(\lambda\) remain obstruction probes, not additional scalar-frame data.

#### Lemma 5: local determinant comparison

For the simple head \(V\), the normal \(2\)-subgroup \(L_Y/K\) acts trivially. Hence its action factors through

\[
H_V=\operatorname{im}(H\to\operatorname{GL}(V)).
\]

Because \(\alpha:T\to H\) is fixed, the induced source action on \(V\) is fixed independently of the lower wild map. The scalar component \(\bar\psi\) does not alter this action.

Consequently the pure determinant zero counts are constants of the fixed boundary stratum:

\[
N_0=
\begin{cases}
2^{d-1}-2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ unramified},\\[2mm]
2^{d-1}+2^{d/2-1},&V\text{ ramified}.
\end{cases}
\tag{111.7}
\]

The candidate Wall calculation and the local Kummer/Hilbert calculation give the same constants. This proposition is therefore already pointwise relative to \(\partial\).

#### Lemma 6: central-cover transform

Let

\[
p:\widetilde Y\twoheadrightarrow Y
\]

be a marked central double cover, and equip it with

\[
q_{\widetilde Y}=q_Yp.
\]

Its central kernel lies in \(\ker q_{\widetilde Y}\). For an exact-image stratum \(J\le Y\), let \(u_\Gamma^\beta(p,J)\) count boundary-framed exact-image maps to \(J\) whose pullback of the cover is split. Then

\[
8u_\Gamma^\beta(p,J)
=
\sum_{\substack{\widetilde J\le p^{-1}(J)\\p(\widetilde J)=J}}
\left|E_\Gamma^\beta(\widetilde J)\right|.
\tag{111.8}
\]

The factor \(8\) is now transparently boundary-safe:

\[
\operatorname{Hom}(\Gamma,C_2)
\simeq
\operatorname{Hom}(\Pi_{\mathrm{el}},C_2)
\]

has order \(2^3=8\), and twisting a lift by any such character changes it only in \(\ker q_{\widetilde Y}\). Hence every twist preserves both components of the boundary equation (111.4).

The exact-image partition and all strict marked-kernel decreases from Turn 109 are unchanged.

### 3. Correct final theorem

The global theorem can now be stated without a source-domain convention:

\[
e_{\Gamma_A}^{\beta}(Y)
=
e_{\Gamma_F}^{\beta}(Y)
\tag{111.9}
\]

for every finite \(\partial\)-framed marked target \(Y\) and every boundary map \(\beta=\beta_{\alpha,\bar\psi}\).

Every one of the six structural lemmas is naturally relative to \(\partial\). Exact-image restriction, quotienting by \(K\) or \(R\), shearing, Fourier pushout and auxiliary central covering all preserve the same equation

\[
q_Yf=\beta b_\Gamma.
\]

The global strong induction therefore remains on the single rank \(|L_Y|\), with no additional scalar-frame or compatibility recursion.

Taking \(E=0\), summing over \(\alpha\), and setting \(L_Y=O_2(Y)\) recovers the official verifier counts. No modification of the submitted relation is indicated.

**ESTABLISHED**: Replaced the separate tame/scalar pair by the single common boundary quotient
\[
\partial=T\times_{C_2}\Pi_{\mathrm{el}}.
\]
Proved that both source groups surject onto this same boundary and reformulated the framed count as one equation over \(\partial\). Audited all six assembled lemmas—terminal pullback, active-block collapse, common-word duality, simple-head determinant/shearing, local determinant comparison, and central-cover transform—and proved that each preserves the corrected boundary exactly. In particular, the factor \(8\) in the central-cover transform remains valid under the fixed scalar frame.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The resulting all-finite-target proof still needs an independent line-by-line review as a standalone manuscript.

**NEXT**: Rewrite the standalone fixed-frame theorem using \(\partial\) and equation (111.4), state the six audited propositions separately, and append the unchanged verifier block for submission.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
